tool_input.summary: Arabia Terra is unlikely to reflect only a single fluvial style; orbital observations in this region commonly require comparing multiple morphologic/stratigraphic expressions (e.g., valley-network erosion, aggradational/inverted channel belts, distributary/fan or delta-like deposits, and basin-fill/lacustrine candidates). In PDS, the most direct way to test “one style vs. many systems” is to combine high-resolution imagery (planform + stratigraphic relationships) with topography (slopes/long profiles) and mineralogy/subsurface context. Below are the key PDS datasets (PDS3 and PDS4 where available) that support that analysis for Arabia Terra on Mars.

candidates.title: VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: CTX (~6 m/pixel) regional imaging is ideal for mapping the spatial diversity of fluvial landforms across Arabia Terra (channel belts vs. valley networks vs. fan/delta morphologies) and for placing higher/lower resolution observations into stratigraphic context.

candidates.title: VOLUME MGSC_0001: MOC DSDP ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: MOC narrow/wide angle images provide additional morphologic coverage for Arabia Terra, useful for recognizing different fluvial styles and erosional vs depositional expressions at intermediate scales.

candidates.title: VOLUME DMGSC_1061: MOC SDP ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Complementary MOC archive that can expand coverage for geomorphic comparisons across Arabia Terra (e.g., different crater basins and intercrater plains) when assessing whether deposits represent multiple depositional systems.

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: MOLA gridded topography supports quantitative tests of fluvial style: long profiles, drainage divides, paleoslope estimates, basin connectivity, and fan/delta gradient constraints in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format
candidates.reasoning: Convenient PDS4-packaged MOLA topography products (IMG) useful for GIS workflows when comparing multiple fluvial systems (regional slopes, catchments, basin morphology) in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
candidates.reasoning: Ready-made MOLA topographic map images (plus IMG) help rapid reconnaissance of Arabia Terra basins and gradients to guide hypotheses about different depositional settings (e.g., fans vs integrated valleys).

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: CRISM targeted RDR spectra can test whether different morphologic deposit types in Arabia Terra have distinct mineralogies (e.g., clay/sulfate/carbonate signatures), supporting interpretation of multiple depositional environments.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected CRISM targeted products (MTRDR) make it easier to co-register mineralogy with CTX/MOLA mapping over Arabia Terra, which is key to discriminating among different fluvial/lacustrine depositional systems.

candidates.title: MRO-M-SHARAD-5-RADARGRAM-V2.0
candidates.reasoning: SHARAD radargrams provide subsurface stratigraphic context (dielectric interfaces, buried units) that can help distinguish stacked depositional packages vs. a single event/style in Arabia Terra basins.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD U.S. Radargrams Bundle
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 SHARAD radargram bundle (U.S. team) provides derived radargram products suitable for integrating with surface geomorphic mapping to evaluate whether Arabia Terra records multiple depositional episodes/systems.

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?

parts.args: {"summary":"Arabia Terra is unlikely to reflect only a single fluvial style; orbital observations in this region commonly require comparing multiple morphologic/stratigraphic expressions (e.g., valley-network erosion, aggradational/inverted channel belts, distributary/fan or delta-like deposits, and basin-fill/lacustrine candidates). In PDS, the most direct way to test “one style vs. many systems” is to combine high-resolution imagery (planform + stratigraphic relationships) with topography (slopes/long profiles) and mineralogy/subsurface context. Below are the key PDS datasets (PDS3 and PDS4 where available) that support that analysis for Arabia Terra on Mars.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-CTX-2-EDR-L0-V1.0","title":"VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"img","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"CTX (~6 m/pixel) regional imaging is ideal for mapping the spatial diversity of fluvial landforms across Arabia Terra (channel belts vs. valley networks vs. fan/delta morphologies) and for placing higher/lower resolution observations into stratigraphic context."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOC-NA/WA-2-DSDP-L0-V1.0","title":"VOLUME MGSC_0001: MOC DSDP ARCHIVE","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"img","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"MOC narrow/wide angle images provide additional morphologic coverage for Arabia Terra, useful for recognizing different fluvial styles and erosional vs depositional expressions at intermediate scales."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOC-NA/WA-2-SDP-L0-V1.0","title":"VOLUME DMGSC_1061: MOC SDP ARCHIVE","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"img","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Complementary MOC archive that can expand coverage for geomorphic comparisons across Arabia Terra (e.g., different crater basins and intercrater plains) when assessing whether deposits represent multiple depositional systems."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","title":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"MOLA gridded topography supports quantitative tests of fluvial style: long profiles, drainage divides, paleoslope estimates, basin connectivity, and fan/delta gradient constraints in Arabia Terra."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_data::1.0","title":"MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Convenient PDS4-packaged MOLA topography products (IMG) useful for GIS workflows when comparing multiple fluvial systems (regional slopes, catchments, basin morphology) in Arabia Terra."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_image::1.0","title":"MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Ready-made MOLA topographic map images (plus IMG) help rapid reconnaissance of Arabia Terra basins and gradients to guide hypotheses about different depositional settings (e.g., fans vs integrated valleys)."},{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0","title":"MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"CRISM targeted RDR spectra can test whether different morphologic deposit types in Arabia Terra have distinct mineralogies (e.g., clay/sulfate/carbonate signatures), supporting interpretation of multiple depositional environments."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_mtrdr::1.0","title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Map-projected CRISM targeted products (MTRDR) make it easier to co-register mineralogy with CTX/MOLA mapping over Arabia Terra, which is key to discriminating among different fluvial/lacustrine depositional systems."},{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-SHARAD-5-RADARGRAM-V2.0","title":"MRO-M-SHARAD-5-RADARGRAM-V2.0","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"SHARAD radargrams provide subsurface stratigraphic context (dielectric interfaces, buried units) that can help distinguish stacked depositional packages vs. a single event/style in Arabia Terra basins."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_sharad_us_radargrams::1.0","title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD U.S. Radargrams Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"PDS4 SHARAD radargram bundle (U.S. team) provides derived radargram products suitable for integrating with surface geomorphic mapping to evaluate whether Arabia Terra records multiple depositional episodes/systems."}]}
